title: Incorrect optimization after large constant shift (`>> 32`) in signed input
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.52+32 (git sha1 9d3d0a433, clang++ 14.0.0-1ubuntu1.1 -fsanitize=address -fsanitize=undefined -fPIC -O3 -O1 -fno-omit-frame-pointer -fno-optimize-sibling-calls -fsanitize=address,undefined)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

This is the original code `test.v`:

```verilog
module top (in1, out2);
    input wire signed [21:18] in1;
    output wire  [24:5] out2;

    assign out2 = in1 >> 32;

endmodule
```

It was optimized using the following commands:

```
read_verilog test.v
opt
proc
write_verilog opt.v
```

The optimized code `opt.v` generated is:

```verilog
/* Generated by Yosys 0.52+32 (git sha1 9d3d0a433, clang++ 14.0.0-1ubuntu1.1 -fsanitize=address -fsanitize=undefined -fPIC -O3 -O1 -fno-omit-frame-pointer -fno-optimize-sibling-calls -fsanitize=address,undefined) */

(* cells_not_processed =  1  *)
(* src = "test.v:2.1-8.10" *)
module top2(in1, out2);                 // changed name from `top` to `top2` for comparison
  (* src = "test.v:3.31-3.34" *)
  input [21:18] in1;
  wire [21:18] in1;
  (* src = "test.v:4.25-4.29" *)
  output [24:5] out2;
  wire [24:5] out2;
  assign out2 = { 4'h0, in1[21], in1[21], in1[21], in1[21], in1[21], in1[21], in1[21], in1[21], in1[21], in1[21], in1[21], in1[21], in1[21], in1[21], in1[21], in1[21] };
endmodule
```

These two versions clearly assign different values to `out2` (they are not equivalent).  
However, according to my understanding, after running `opt; proc`, the generated code should be functionally equivalent to the original code.

These two versions also fail to pass the following formal verification script:

```yosys
read_verilog test.v
read_verilog opt.v
miter -equiv -make_assert -make_outputs top top2 miter
flatten miter
sat -verify -prove-asserts -show-inputs -show-outputs miter
```

The output of the script is:

```
SAT proof finished - model found: FAIL!

   ______                   ___       ___       _ _            _ _ 
  (_____ \                 / __)     / __)     (_) |          | | |
   _____) )___ ___   ___ _| |__    _| |__ _____ _| | _____  __| | |
  |  ____/ ___) _ \ / _ (_   __)  (_   __|____ | | || ___ |/ _  |_|
  | |   | |  | |_| | |_| || |       | |  / ___ | | || ____( (_| |_ 
  |_|   |_|   \___/ \___/ |_|       |_|  \_____|_|\_)_____)\____|_|


  Signal Name             Dec       Hex                     Bin
  --------------- ----------- --------- -----------------------
  \gate_out2            65535      ffff    00001111111111111111
  \gold_out2                0         0    00000000000000000000
  \in_in1                   8         8                    1000
  \trigger                  1         1                       1

ERROR: Called with -verify and proof did fail!
```

### Expected Behavior

The functionality of the code before and after optimization should be consisten

### Actual Behavior

The functionality of the code before and after optimization is inconsistent
code_blocks:
read_verilog test.v
read_verilog opt.v
miter -equiv -make_assert -make_outputs top top2 miter
flatten miter
sat -verify -prove-asserts -show-inputs -show-outputs miter
